task_completion.description: Model at least 6 distinct solar panels on the roof of the building.
task_completion.visual_evidence: 3D viewport showing the building model with at least 6 distinct rectangular, panel-like objects placed on its roof.

task_completion.description: Classify the modeled panels as IfcSolarDevice entities.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Object properties, IFC classification panel, or outliner showing the selected panel(s) assigned the IFC class 'IfcSolarDevice'.

task_completion.description: Create a new IfcSystem representing the electrical generation system with 'Solar' or 'PV' in the name.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Outliner, project tree, or system manager panel showing a new system created with a name containing 'Solar' or 'PV' (e.g., 'Solar PV Array').

task_completion.description: Assign all 6 solar panels to the newly created electrical generation system.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Outliner or system hierarchy showing the 6 solar panel objects nested under, linked to, or grouped within the newly created Solar/PV system.

task_completion.description: Add a custom property to either the panels or the system to define its capacity, with the name containing 'Power', 'Watt', or 'Capacity'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Custom properties, property sets (Psets), or IFC attributes panel showing a property name containing 'Power', 'Watt', or 'Capacity' (e.g., NominalPower) with an assigned value (e.g., 400W).

task_completion.description: Save the enriched project as a new IFC file to /home/ga/BIMProjects/fzk_solar_retrofit.ifc.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Save dialog, file manager, terminal verification, or application title bar showing the file saved as 'fzk_solar_retrofit.ifc' in the '/home/ga/BIMProjects/' directory.

integrity.description: Agent used the BIM software GUI to perform the modeling and classification.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with a 3D BIM application's graphical user interface (e.g., BlenderBIM, FreeCAD) rather than writing a Python script in a text editor or terminal.

integrity.description: Original building elements were not deleted.
integrity.visual_evidence: The original FZK-Haus building structure (walls, roof, windows, etc.) remains visible in the 3D viewport and outliner alongside the new solar panels.

integrity.description: Property and system creation was done via genuine software interaction.
integrity.visual_evidence: UI panels for property creation, IFC classification, and system management are actively used and visible in the screenshots.
